description:
## Overview

Define where the device sends data by configuring the broker connection parameters including URL, security, and topics.

## Commands

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `config_endpoint` | Configure broker URL, port, protocol, credentials, topics, and TLS settings |
| `get_endpoint_config` | Retrieve current endpoint configuration |

## Configuration

Includes:
- Broker URL and port
- Publish/subscribe topics
- QoS levels
- Keep-alive interval
- Authentication credentials
- Reconnect behavior and timeouts
- TLS/certificate settings

post.description:
The config_endpoint command configures or updates endpoint definitions used by the reader.

This command allows you to configure:
- Endpoint identity and type
- Protocol and connection parameters (URL, port, QoS, tenant)
- Activation status
- Protocol-specific settings such as keep-alive, reconnect behavior, and topic mapping

Use this command to:
- Add new endpoint configurations
- Update existing endpoint settings
- Delete endpoints that are no longer required
- Control which endpoint is active for communication

### Command Details

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Pattern Name | Endpoint Configuration Management |
| Communication Type | Bidirectional (Cloud to Device, Device to Cloud) |
| Applies To | RFD40 Series, RFD90 Series |
| Related Commands | [get_endpoint_config](#op-get-endpoint-config), [config_events](#op-config-events), [set_config](#op-set-config), [reboot](#op-reboot) |
| Required Request Fields | command, requestId, epConfig |
| Supported Operations | add, update, delete |
| Supported Types | Endpoint types: MGMT, MGMT_EVT, CTRL, DATA1, DATA2, SOTI, MDM; verification types: NONE, VERIFY_PEER, VERIFY_HOST, VERIFY_HOST_PEER |
| Supported Profiles | Endpoint configuration profiles with protocol-specific connection and topic settings |
| Supported Protocols | MQTT, MQTT_WS, MQTT_WSS, MQTT_TLS, TCP, HTTP, HTTPS, WS, WSS, AWS, AZURE, HID |
| Supported API Versions | V1.0, V1.1 |

command.description: Command name for endpoint configuration operations.

requestId.description: Unique request identifier used to correlate this command with its response.

epConfig.title: epConfig
epConfig.description: Endpoint configuration payload containing the requested operation and endpoint settings.

operation.description: Operation to perform on the endpoint definition.

configuration.description: Endpoint settings used for the requested operation.

endpointName.description: Identifies the name of the endpoint, which determines its purpose or function.

epType.description: Specifies the type of the endpoint.

protocol.description: Defines the communication protocol used by the endpoint.

activate.description: Set to true to mark this endpoint active.

url.description: Specifies the URL associated with the endpoint.

verificationType.description: Specifies the type of verification used for establishing secure connections.

port.description: Specifies the port number used for communication.

qosCommon.description: Defines the Quality of Service (QoS) level for the endpoint.

tenantId.description: Tenant identifier associated with this endpoint configuration.

mqttParams.description: MQTT-specific parameters for configuring the MQTT connection.

keepAlive.description: Specifies the keep-alive interval in seconds when client is idle.

cleanSession.description: Indicates whether to start a clean session for the MQTT client.

reconnectDelayMin.description: Specifies the minimum delay in seconds before reconnecting when a connection is lost.

reconnectDelayMax.description: Specifies the maximum delay in seconds before reconnecting when a connection is lost.

clientId.description: Specifies the client ID used for MQTT connections.

username.description: The username for authentication in MQTT connections.

password.description: The password for authentication in MQTT connections.

items.description: One topic configuration entry for published messages.

topic.description: Specifies the topic to publish messages to. Supports up to 3 publish topics.

qos.description: Defines the QoS level for publishing messages.

retain.description: Indicates whether the message should be retained by the broker.

items.description: One topic configuration entry for subscribed messages.

topic.description: Specifies the topic to subscribe to. Supports up to 1 subscribe topic.

qos.description: Defines the QoS level for subscribing to messages.

retain.description: Indicates whether the subscription retains messages.

securityParams.description: Security material and certificate settings used by the endpoint connection.

format.description: Specifies the format of the security certificates. Currently, only PEM is supported.

algorithm.description: Specifies the algorithm used for encryption.

caCertificateFile.description: Specifies the file containing the CA certificates.

clientCert.description: Specifies the file containing the client certificate.

clientKey.description: Specifies the file containing the client private key.

eventConfiguration.title: eventConfiguration
eventConfiguration.description: Event configuration associated with this endpoint.

antenna.description: Set to true to enable antenna-related events.

terminalConnection.description: Set to true to enable terminal connection and disconnection events.

firmwareUpdate.description: Set to true to enable firmware update events.

gpi.description: Set to true to enable GPI events.

network.description: Set to true to enable network events.

exceptions.description: Set to true to enable exception events.

ntp.description: Set to true to enable NTP-related events.

userApp.description: Set to true to enable user application events.

heartbeat.description: Set to true to enable heartbeat events.

power.description: Set to true to enable power source events.

battery.description: Set to true to enable battery events.

temperature.description: Set to true to enable temperature events.

fileDownload.description: Set to true to enable file download events.

cpuUsage.description: Set to true to enable CPU usage alerts.

flashUsage.description: Set to true to enable flash usage alerts.

ramUsage.description: Set to true to enable RAM usage alerts.

heartbeatConfiguration.description: Heartbeat payload options and interval settings.

inventoryStatus.description: Set to true to include inventory status in heartbeat messages.

batteryStatus.description: Set to true to include battery status in heartbeat messages.

userApps.description: Set to true to include user app status in heartbeat messages.

cpuThreshold.description: CPU usage percentage above which a CPU alert is triggered.

ramThreshold.description: RAM usage percentage above which a RAM alert is triggered.

flashThreshold.description: Flash usage percentage above which a flash alert is triggered.

temperatureThreshold.description: Temperature value above which a temperature alert is triggered.

Add MDM endpoint.description: Payload to add a new MDM endpoint configuration

Delete endpoint.description: Payload to delete an existing endpoint configuration

Add data endpoint.description: Payload to add a new data endpoint configuration

Add control endpoint.description: Payload to add a new control endpoint with publish/subscribe topics

command.description: Echoes the command name so clients can map this response to the config_endpoint request.

requestId.description: Request identifier copied from the command, used for request and response correlation.

apiVersion.description: API version used to interpret this response payload.

response.title: response
response.description: Execution result object containing status code and result description.

code.description:
Response code indicating success or failure.

- 0 — Success
- 10 — Configuration already exist
- 23 — Invalid enum value
- 25 — Max 3 publish topics exceeded
- 26 — Max 1 subscribe topic exceeded
- 27 — Invalid tenant ID length

description.description: Human-readable result message that explains the response code.

x-error-codes.cause: An endpoint or configuration with the same name already exists
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Delete the existing configuration first or use a different name

x-error-codes.cause: A field value does not match any of the allowed enum options
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the schema for allowed values and correct the field

x-error-codes.cause: More than 3 publish topics were specified in the endpoint configuration
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Reduce the number of publish topics to 3 or fewer

x-error-codes.cause: More than 1 subscribe topic was specified in the endpoint configuration
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Use only 1 subscribe topic per endpoint

x-error-codes.cause: The tenant ID exceeds the maximum allowed character length
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Shorten the tenant ID to within the allowed limit

post.description:
The get_endpoint_config command retrieves endpoint configuration details stored on the reader.

This command returns:
- Configured endpoint definitions on the reader
- Active endpoint routing and transport settings
- Saved endpoint names and endpoint metadata
- Response metadata for query execution

Use this command to:
- Inspect active endpoint connectivity configuration
- Retrieve settings for a specific endpoint by name
- Audit saved endpoint inventory on the device

No additional command-specific payload fields are required beyond the standard request envelope fields.

### Command Details

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Pattern Name | Endpoint Configuration Query |
| Communication Type | Bidirectional (Cloud to Device, Device to Cloud) |
| Applies To | RFD40 Series, RFD90 Series |
| Related Commands | [config_endpoint](#op-config-endpoint), [config_events](#op-config-events), [get_config](#op-get-config) |
| Required Request Fields | command, requestId |
| Supported Operations | Retrieve all endpoint configuration or query a specific endpoint by name |
| Supported Response Sections | epDetails, response |
| Supported API Versions | V1.0, V1.1 |

command.description: Command name for requesting endpoint configuration details.

requestId.description: Unique request identifier used to correlate this command with its response.

endpointDetails.title: endpointDetails
endpointDetails.description: Optional object used to request a specific endpoint configuration by endpoint name.

endpointName.description: Name of the endpoint to query. If omitted, all active and saved endpoints are returned.

Active endpoint config.description: Payload to get active endpoint configuration details with all saved endpoint names

Specific endpoint config.description: Payload to get the specified endpoint configurations

command.description: Echoes the command name so clients can map this response to the get_endpoint_config request.

requestId.description: Request identifier copied from the command, used for request and response correlation.

apiVersion.description: API version used to interpret this response payload.

epDetails.title: endpointResponse
epDetails.description: Endpoint configuration details, including active endpoint settings and saved endpoint names.

activeEndpoints.title: epList
activeEndpoints.description: A reference to the configuration details of currently active endpoints.

items.title: epConfig
items.description: The configuration details for the endpoint, including MQTT parameters and security settings.

configuration.description: Endpoint configuration details including connection, protocol, and optional security settings.

endpointName.description: The name of the endpoint, e.g., "MDM" for Mobile Device Management.

epType.description: The type of endpoint, specifying its functionality (e.g., Management, Control, etc.).

protocol.description: The communication protocol used by the endpoint, such as MQTT or HTTP.

activate.description: Indicates whether the endpoint is activated (true or false).

url.description: The URL of the endpoint, specifying its address.

verificationType.description: The type of verification to be used for the endpoint connection.

port.description: The port number used for the connection.

qosCommon.description: The Quality of Service (QoS) level for message delivery.

mqttParams.description: MQTT-specific parameters for configuring the MQTT connection.

keepAlive.description: The keep-alive interval for the MQTT connection.

cleanSession.description: Indicates whether the MQTT session should be clean (true or false).

reconnectDelayMin.description: Minimum delay before reconnecting in case of disconnection.

reconnectDelayMax.description: Maximum delay before reconnecting in case of disconnection.

clientId.description: The client ID used for the MQTT connection.

username.description: The username for authenticating the MQTT connection.

password.description: The password for authenticating the MQTT connection.

topic.description: The topic to publish messages to.

qos.description: The QoS level for the published topic.

retain.description: Indicates whether the message should be retained on the broker.

publishTopics.description: A list of topics to which messages will be published.

topic.description: The topic to which the client will subscribe.

qos.description: The QoS level for the subscription.

retain.description: Indicates whether the message should be retained.

subscribeTopics.description: A list of topics to which the client will subscribe.

format.description: The format of the security certificate (e.g., PEM or PFX).

caCertificateFile.description: The CA certificate file for establishing trust.

clientCert.description: The client certificate file for authentication.

clientKey.description: The client key file for authentication.

securityParams.description: Security parameters for the endpoint connection.

eventConfiguration.title: eventConfiguration
eventConfiguration.description: Reference to the event configuration payload.

antenna.description: Set to true to enable antenna-related events.

terminalConnection.description: Set to true to enable terminal connection and disconnection events.

firmwareUpdate.description: Set to true to enable firmware update events.

gpi.description: Set to true to enable GPI events.

network.description: Set to true to enable network events.

exceptions.description: Set to true to enable exception events.

ntp.description: Set to true to enable NTP-related events.

userApp.description: Set to true to enable user application events.

heartbeat.description: Set to true to enable heartbeat events.

power.description: Set to true to enable power source events.

battery.description: Set to true to enable battery events.

temperature.description: Set to true to enable temperature events.

fileDownload.description: Set to true to enable file download events.

cpuUsage.description: Set to true to enable CPU usage alerts.

flashUsage.description: Set to true to enable flash usage alerts.

ramUsage.description: Set to true to enable RAM usage alerts.

heartbeatConfiguration.description: Heartbeat payload options and interval settings.

inventoryStatus.description: Set to true to include inventory status in heartbeat messages.

batteryStatus.description: Set to true to include battery status in heartbeat messages.

userApps.description: Set to true to include user app status in heartbeat messages.

cpuThreshold.description: CPU usage percentage above which a CPU alert is triggered.

ramThreshold.description: RAM usage percentage above which a RAM alert is triggered.

flashThreshold.description: Flash usage percentage above which a flash alert is triggered.

temperatureThreshold.description: Temperature value above which a temperature alert is triggered.

savedEndpoints.title: savedEpconfigs
savedEndpoints.description: A reference to the configuration details of saved endpoint configurations.

epNames.description: Array of saved endpoint names available on the device.

response.title: response
response.description: Execution result object containing status code and result description.

code.description:
Response code indicating success or failure.

- 0 — Success
- 3 — Not able to retrieve information

description.description: Human-readable result message that explains the response code.

Active endpoint config.description: Payload to get active endpoint configuration details with all saved endpoint names

Specific endpoint config.description: Payload to get the specified endpoint configurations

x-error-codes.cause: The device could not gather the requested information at this time
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Retry the command after a short delay; if persistent, reboot the device
